user_request: Draft a suit for damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. Reproduce the defamatory statements in the body of the plaint. Plead malice, publication to third parties, loss of goodwill, and mental agony. Claim Rs.10,00,000/- as damages. Include prayer for permanent injunction restraining further publication on Facebook or any other social media platform. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court. Plead limitation correctly for defamation suits. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks damages for defamation against a Defendant who published false and malicious allegations on Facebook, damaging the Plaintiff's business reputation. The suit includes claims for loss of goodwill, mental agony, and a permanent injunction against further publication.

missing_fields: Date of publication of defamatory content
Specific content of the defamatory statements
assumptions: Jurisdiction lies with the District Court based on Plaintiff's residence or place of business
Defamatory content is accessible to the public on Facebook

queries: cause of action accrual defamation continuing

detriment of the other party.61
In considering an application for leave to amend pleadings, subsequent events can be taken into account.62

draft_artifacts.title: PLAINT FOR DAMAGES FOR DEFAMATION
draft_artifacts.text:
IN THE DISTRICT COURT AT {{PLACE}}, {{STATE}}

PLAINT SUIT NO. ______ OF 20__

PLAINTIFF: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

DEFENDANT: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, resident of {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

CAUSE OF ACTION AND JURISDICTION
1. The Plaintiff is a resident and/or carries on business within the jurisdiction of this Court. This suit is for defamation caused by false and malicious statements allegedly published on Facebook by the Defendant, a social media platform accessible in India, which statements have harmed the Plaintiff’s business reputation and goodwill.

2. The present cause of action arises from the publication on Facebook on date(s) to be verified, namely {{FIRST_PUBLICATION_DATE}}, and continuing publication thereafter by the Defendant. The Plaintiff reproduces the defamatory statements in the body of this plaint at the appropriate place as Annexure-1 and states that these statements are false, malicious and defamatory. The publication has been communicated to third parties.

3. Jurisdiction and venue: This Hon’ble Court has jurisdiction to entertain and try this suit as the cause of action arises within this jurisdiction and the subject matter involves defamation published on a platform accessible within India; the Plaintiff’s business and place of business (as stated in the facts) fall within the territorial jurisdiction of this Court.

PARTICULARS OF DEFAMATORY PUBLICATION AND REPRODUCTION OF STATEMENTS
4. The defamatory statements published on Facebook by the Defendant are: {{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS}}. The Plaintiff repeats and reproduces the exact words or substantially similar words in Annexure-1 for the purpose of this plaint.

5. The publication was made with malice and/or reckless disregard for the truth and was communicated to third parties on Facebook and/or other social media platforms.

6. The Plaintiff has suffered and continues to suffer loss of goodwill and mental agony due to the publication of the false statements. The damage to business reputation and loss of customers/clients has caused monetary and non-monetary injury.

CAUSES OF ACTION AND MALICE
7. The Defendant published false and malicious statements about the Plaintiff on Facebook, with or without knowledge of their falsity, or with reckless disregard for the truth, thereby imputing or implying the Plaintiff’s impropriety in business and otherwise harming the Plaintiff’s reputation among customers, suppliers and the general public.

8. Publication to third parties: The statements were published to the public at large and/or a substantial section of the public, including customers and potential customers of the Plaintiff, causing reputational harm and loss of business goodwill.

9. Damages sought: The Plaintiff prays for damages in the sum of Rs. 10,00,000 (Rupees ten lacs) for defamation, loss of goodwill, and mental agony.

RELIEF PRAYED FOR
10. A permanent injunction restraining the Defendant from publishing any defamatory statements about the Plaintiff on Facebook or on any other social media platform and from circulating or disseminating the same to third parties.

11. Such further or other reliefs as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper.

CAUSE OF ACTION – ACCRUAL, CONTINUING NATURE AND NOTICE
12. Accrual of cause of action: The cause of action arises on the date of first publication of the defaming statements, namely {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}; the right to sue accrued on that date.

13. Continuity of action: The cause of action is of a continuing nature in that further defamatory publications on Facebook and other social media platforms may occur unless restrained by this Court, and the Plaintiff seeks an order to prevent ongoing and future publication.

14. Notice/Demand: To the extent any notice is required prior to filing, the existence and date of such notice is {{NOTICE_DATE}}; verification and details to be completed at filing, if applicable.

LIMITATION
15. The Plaintiff submits that the suit is commenced within the period prescribed for civil defamation suits; the applicable limitation article/period to be verified at the time of filing and cited as {{LIMITATION_ARTICLE}} and {{LIMITATION_PERIOD}} as per the governing statute and rules in force. (Noting that the exact article/period is to be confirmed in accordance with the Court's archival provisions.)

VALUATION, COURT FEE AND JURISDICTIONAL SUM
16. Suit value for the purpose of Court Fee: Rs. 10,00,000 (Rupees ten Lacs).

17. Court fee: {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} (to be verified/adjusted at the time of filing) under the applicable Act/Schedule.

VERIFICATION
18. I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above named, do hereby verify that the contents of this plaint are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief.

ANNEXURES AND LIST OF DOCUMENTS
Annexure-1: Copy of the defamatory statements published on Facebook (printout/screenshot as available).
Annexure-2: Evidence of publication to third parties (screenshots, links, etc.).
Annexure-3: Any other relevant documents in support of the Plaintiff’s case.

List of Documents / Enclosures
1. Annexure-1: Copy of defamatory statements (Facebook post).
2. Annexure-2: Evidence of publication to third parties.
3. Annexure-3: Additional documents (if any).

PRAYER
In the premises, it is most respectfully prayed that the suit be decreed in terms of prayers (1) to (3) above and such other relief as this Court may deem fit and proper.

DRAFTING NOTES AND placeholders used:
- This draft uses placeholders for missing factual details (names, addresses, dates, etc.) as {{PLACEHOLDER_NAME}} to be filled before filing.
- Placeholder for LIMITATION_ARTICLE and LIMITATION_PERIOD to be verified against the applicable statute and rules in force.
- Placeholder for COURT_FEE_AMOUNT to be computed as per COURT_FEE_CONTEXT at the time of filing.
- Defamatory statements are reproduced using a placeholder {{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS}} to be inserted from the records or annexed material.

VERIFICATION
I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, do hereby verify that the contents of the plaint are true to my knowledge and belief.

DECLARATION
A true copy of the plaint along with annexures is filed in compliance with the Civil Procedure Code and the Rules of this Court.

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

2. 3. This Hon’ble Court has territorial and pecuniary jurisdiction to entertain and try the present suit in view of the facts stated in paragraphs 1–2 above and as the suit valuation for the purpose of jurisdiction is Rs. 10,00,000/-.

4. On {{FIRST_PUBLICATION_DATE}}, the Defendant published, on the social media platform “Facebook”, false, malicious and defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff and the Plaintiff’s business. 5. The exact defamatory statements (verbatim) as published by the Defendant on Facebook are reproduced below for the purposes of this plaint:

   (a) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_1}}”

   (c) “{{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENT_3}}”

7. The Plaintiff reserves the right to provide further particulars and additional names as may be discovered.

9. As a direct consequence of the Defendant’s defamatory publication, the Plaintiff has suffered:
   (i) loss of goodwill and reputation;
   (ii) adverse impact on business relations and customer confidence;
   (iii) mental agony, humiliation and stress.

III. DAMAGES
10. The Plaintiff claims damages quantified at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only) on account of harm to reputation and business goodwill and for mental agony and suffering caused by the Defendant’s acts.

IV. PERMANENT INJUNCTION
11. The Defendant has not removed the Impugned Statements and/or continues to circulate, share or cause circulation of the same, and there is a real and imminent apprehension that the Defendant will repeat and/or further publish defamatory matter against the Plaintiff on Facebook and/or other social media platforms unless restrained by an order of this Hon’ble Court.

V. CAUSE OF ACTION (ACCRUAL AND DATES)
12. The cause of action first arose on {{FIRST_PUBLICATION_DATE}} when the Defendant first published the Impugned Statements on Facebook and the Plaintiff’s right to sue accrued on that date.

13. The cause of action further arose and continues to arise on such subsequent dates when the Impugned Statements remained accessible and were viewed, shared and/or commented upon by third parties, including on {{SUBSEQUENT_PUBLICATION_OR_ACCESS_DATES}}, thereby continuing the harm to the Plaintiff’s reputation and business.

14. The present suit, being a suit for compensation for defamation/libel, is filed within the prescribed period of limitation of one year from the date when the right to sue first accrued, i.e., from {{FIRST_PUBLICATION_DATE}}.

VII. VALUATION AND COURT FEE
15. For the purposes of pecuniary jurisdiction and court fee, the suit is valued at Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only), being the amount of damages claimed.

In view of the facts and circumstances stated above, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon’ble Court may be pleased to:

A. Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for recovery of damages of Rs. 10,00,000/- (Rupees Ten Lakhs only);

B. Pass a decree of permanent injunction restraining the Defendant, his/her agents, associates and any person acting through or under the Defendant from publishing, posting, sharing, circulating or otherwise disseminating any defamatory statements concerning the Plaintiff on Facebook or any other social media platform;

C. Award costs of the suit in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant; and

D. Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon’ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

1. Annexure-1: Printouts/screenshots of the Facebook post(s)/publication containing the Impugned Statements.
2. Annexure-2: Screenshots/links/comments/shares evidencing publication/communication to third parties.
3. Annexure-3: Any other documents relied upon by the Plaintiff (to be filed).

Verified at {{PLACE}} on this ___ day of ______ 20__.

Plaintiff

blocking_issues.issue: Limitation for defamation not pleaded correctly; draft leaves limitation article/period as placeholders instead of pleading the applicable limitation period for civil defamation (libel) suits.
blocking_issues.fix: Replace the limitation paragraph to plead the limitation period as 1 year for compensation for libel/defamation, and tie it to the first date of publication ({{FIRST_PUBLICATION_DATE}} / {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}). If jurisdictional article number cannot be verified from provided RAG_CHUNKS, avoid citing a specific Article number; plead the period and accrual factually and reserve the article number as a placeholder only if necessary.

blocking_issues.issue: Cause of action paragraph asserts continuing cause of action based on possibility of future publications; this is not a proper continuing cause of action pleading and risks incorrect limitation anchoring. Continuing wrong must be pleaded based on continuing publication/continued availability of the impugned post(s), not mere apprehension of future posts.
blocking_issues.fix: Amend cause of action paragraph to: (i) anchor accrual to the first publication date; (ii) plead that the post(s) continued to remain accessible/available and were viewed/shared on dates to be specified ({{LAST_ACCESSED_DATE}} / {{VIEWED_BY_THIRD_PARTIES_DATES}}) if true; (iii) keep injunction relief on apprehension of repetition, but do not label limitation as continuously extending merely due to apprehension.
blocking_issues.location: Paragraphs 12–13 (CAUSE OF ACTION – ACCRUAL, CONTINUING NATURE)

blocking_issues.issue: Court fee amount stated without verified rate × value computation; jurisdiction likely requires ad valorem court fee on the damages claim, but draft uses {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} without computation and COURT_FEE_CONTEXT provides no rate.
blocking_issues.fix: Either compute court fee using the applicable state court-fee schedule (rate × Rs.10,00,000) once confirmed, or retain a placeholder but explicitly state it is to be paid as per the applicable State Court Fees Act on the suit valuation of Rs.10,00,000 and remove any implied finality. Use {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} placeholder with a clear reason.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Document/annexure reference inconsistency: body says defamatory statements are in ‘Annexure-1’ and also uses {{DEFAMATORY_STATEMENTS}} in para 4; request requires reproducing the defamatory statements in the body, not only via annexure.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Either reproduce the statements verbatim in para 4 (and optionally also annex them), or if too lengthy, reproduce material portions in the body and annex full screenshots. Ensure Annexure-1 reference remains consistent.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Prayer clause references ‘prayers (1) to (3) above’ though relief list is numbered 10–11 and there is no explicit damages prayer item separately enumerated in the PRAYER section.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Rewrite PRAYER section with clear numbered prayers: (i) damages Rs.10,00,000 with interest (if sought), (ii) permanent injunction, (iii) costs, (iv) other reliefs.

non_blocking_issues.issue: Duplicate verification appears twice and includes ‘DRAFTING NOTES’ inside the plaint body; these should not be part of a filed pleading.
non_blocking_issues.fix: Remove drafting notes from the plaint text and keep a single verification clause in the proper format as per local rules.

unsupported_statements.statement: “To the extent any notice is required prior to filing…”
unsupported_statements.reason: No factual basis provided for any statutory/mandatory pre-suit notice requirement in a private defamation damages suit; this may confuse procedure. Keep only if a specific notice was actually issued and is being relied upon factually.

unsupported_statements.statement: “This Hon’ble Court has jurisdiction … as the cause of action arises within this jurisdiction … published on a platform accessible within India”
unsupported_statements.reason: Mere accessibility ‘within India’ is insufficient to plead territorial jurisdiction for a District Court; needs facts showing viewing/publication/impact within the court’s territorial limits (e.g., plaintiff’s business located here; customers in this district saw the post; defendant resides/works here; post targeted this locality).
